title: rosa × andegavensis var. agrestina
intro: Rosa × andegavensis var. agrestina, also known as the wild rose of Andegavensis, is a subspecies of wild rose that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rosa × andegavensis var. agrestina include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical creams and salves to promote skin health and wound healing.
